# Wrap-up {background-image="assets/symbol_clockwork.png" background-opacity="0.3" background-size="cover" background-color="#2d4059"}

The **discipline** matters more than the specific tools.

## Same discipline, every time

::: {.incremental}
1. **Find the dominant cost** — logging, then profilers
2. **Classify the bottleneck** — algorithmic? interpreter overhead? memory layout?
3. **Change one thing** — the thing the evidence points to
4. **Verify on the same workload** — timeit, Google Benchmark, or just timestamps
:::

::: {.fragment}
::: {.platypus-tip}
pybind11 is not "make it fast".
Google Benchmark is not "time it nicely".
Both are part of a measurement-driven workflow.
:::
:::

## Further reading {.smaller}

::: {.platypus-book}
- **[Performance Analysis and Tuning on Modern CPUs](https://book.easyperf.net/perf_book)** — Denis Bakhvalov (CC0, free). Companion exercises: [perf-ninja](https://github.com/dendibakh/perf-ninja).
- **[What Every Programmer Should Know About Memory](https://people.freebsd.org/~lstewart/articles/cpumemory.pdf)** — Ulrich Drepper (2007). The classic on memory hierarchy and caches.
- **[Data Structures in Practice](https://github.com/data-structures-in-practice/data-structures-in-practice-public)** — Danny Jiang (CC BY 4.0). Hardware-aware data structures, backed by `perf` benchmarks.
:::

::: {.platypus-video}
- **[Catching Cache: A Detective Story](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VhKq0nzPTh0)** — Michelle Nguyen (Bloomberg, ~42 min). Why `vector` beats `unordered_map` by 10×.
- **[Matrix Multiplication Optimization](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GHctcSBd6Z4)** — Alexia Salah (Codeplay, ~46 min). 150× speedup: loop order → tiling → SIMD → cache-aware design.
- **[Want fast C++? Know your hardware!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BP6NxVxDQIs)** — Timur Doumler (CppCon 2016, ~60 min). Caches, branch prediction, false sharing, denormals.
:::

## Tool documentation

| | |
|---|---|
| [C++ Core Guidelines](https://isocpp.github.io/CppCoreGuidelines/CppCoreGuidelines) | Parameter passing, copy semantics, best practices |
| [Google Benchmark](https://github.com/google/benchmark) | Microbenchmarking framework for C++ |
| [pybind11 Documentation](https://pybind11.readthedocs.io) | C++/Python bindings — reference & tutorial |
| [Course Material Repository](https://github.com/tubs-alg/algorithm-engineering-material-ss2026) | Slides, examples, and exercises |

## Concepts, not tools {.smaller}

| Tool | Concept | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| `logging` | Structured observability | `loguru`, `spdlog` (C++) |
| Scalene | **Sampling** profiler (Python) | `py-spy`, VTune |
| `perf` | **Sampling** profiler (C++) | VTune, `Instruments` (macOS) |
| cProfile | **Tracing** profiler | `yappi`, `gprof` |
| pybind11 | Native bindings for Python | `nanobind`, `cython`, `PyO3` |
| `timeit` | Microbenchmarking (Python) | `pytest-benchmark` |
| Google Benchmark | Microbenchmarking (C++) | `Catch2`, `criterion` (Rust) |
